title: AWeber vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare AWeber and Omnisend email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, ratings, and which tool fits your needs.
h1: AWeber vs Omnisend: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: AWeber and Omnisend are both popular email marketing platforms with free tiers, but they cater to different audiences. AWeber focuses on simplicity for bloggers and solopreneurs, while Omnisend specializes in omnichannel marketing for e-commerce businesses. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns with your marketing goals and budget.
choose_a_if: You're a blogger or solopreneur seeking an easy-to-use email platform
You need landing page builder functionality integrated into your email tool
You want the largest free tier with 500 subscribers included
You prefer a simpler, focused feature set without SMS/push notifications
You value the 30% affiliate commission for recurring sales
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce business needing email, SMS, and push notifications
You want advanced omnichannel automation and pre-built workflows
You need sophisticated segmentation and a product picker for dynamic content
You prioritize higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.5+ G2, 4.6+ Trustpilot)
You're looking for a platform purpose-built for online stores

faq.a: It depends on your use case. AWeber is better for bloggers and solopreneurs who want simplicity and a larger free tier, while Omnisend excels for e-commerce businesses needing omnichannel marketing with SMS and push notifications. Omnisend has higher customer ratings overall, but AWeber's straightforward approach appeals to users who don't need advanced features.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, AWeber or Omnisend?
faq.a: Both platforms have similar paid pricing starting around $15-16/month, making them nearly identical in cost. However, AWeber offers a better free tier with 500 subscribers compared to Omnisend's 250. For budget-conscious users, AWeber's free plan provides more value before upgrading.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to AWeber?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list imports and exports, making migration straightforward. Most email marketing tools allow you to export your subscriber data in standard formats like CSV. The transition is relatively simple, though you may lose any SMS or push notification data since AWeber doesn't offer those features.
